Package org.mitre.synthea.helpers

Class TransitionMetrics.Metric

java.lang.Object
org.mitre.synthea.helpers.TransitionMetrics.Metric
Enclosing class:
TransitionMetrics
public static class TransitionMetrics.Metric extends Object
Helper class to track the metrics of a single State.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    final AtomicInteger
    current
    Number of people that are "currently" in that state.
    final Map<String,AtomicInteger>
    destinations
    Tracker for what states this state transitions to.
    final AtomicLong
    duration
    Total length of time (ms) people were in this state.
    final AtomicInteger
    entered
    Number of times the state was entered.
    final AtomicInteger
    population
    Number of people that ever his this state.

  • Field Details

    • entered

      public final AtomicInteger entered
      Number of times the state was entered.

    • duration

      public final AtomicLong duration
      Total length of time (ms) people were in this state.

    • population

      public final AtomicInteger population
      Number of people that ever his this state.

    • current

      public final AtomicInteger current
      Number of people that are "currently" in that state.

    • destinations

      public final Map<String,AtomicInteger> destinations
      Tracker for what states this state transitions to. Key: state that this state transitioned to. Value: number of times

  • Constructor Details

    • Metric

      public Metric()

  • Method Details

    • exit

      public void exit(String destination, long duration)
      Helper function to increment the count for a destination state.
      Parameters:
      destination - Target state that was transitioned to.
      duration - The time in milliseconds spent within the state.

    • enter

      public void enter(boolean firstTime)
      Helper function to increment the counts when a state is entered.
      Parameters:
      firstTime - Whether or not this state was previously entered.
